toolUseResult.originalFile:
Skip to content
ZodiacID
  • Home
  • Products
  • Samples
  • About
  • FAQ
  • Contact
Home/About

About ZodiacID

Astronomical precision meets psychological interpretation.

The Story

ZodiacID started with a question: what if natal chart readings had the same precision as the astronomical calculations behind them?

Most birth chart readings rely on generic Sun-sign descriptions or brief template paragraphs. We took a different approach. We built a multi-stage analytical pipeline that starts with Astrolog 7.80 — a professional astronomical engine powered by Swiss Ephemeris — computing exact positions for 20 celestial bodies, mapping all 9 aspect types with precise orbs down to the arcminute, and calculating full Placidus house placements.

But raw chart data is only the beginning. We extract each chart’s unique statistical signature: planetary weights by sign and house, elemental and modal distribution, aspect geometry, mutual receptions, and dispositorship chains. This data feeds into 13 specialized analysis modules — each one grounded in the interpretive frameworks of Liz Greene (psychological astrology), Robert Hand (systematic aspect analysis), and Steven Forrest (evolutionary astrology).

Every section is written individually for your specific placements, cross-referencing aspects with house rulers and planetary dignity. The result is a 40+ page report that reads like it was written by someone who actually studied your chart — because that is exactly what happens.

Our Approach

Every report starts with your exact birth data — date, time, and city. We geocode your birth location and compute the precise position of each celestial body at your moment of birth. From this raw astronomical data we derive your complete chart architecture: planetary dignities (domicile, detriment, exaltation, fall), house rulership chains, mutual receptions, and the specific aspect web connecting all 20 bodies.

Your chart is then analyzed through 13 specialized modules: from core identity (Sun, Ascendant, chart ruler) through emotional architecture (Moon, 4th house, Cancer placements) to career dynamics (Midheaven, Saturn aspects, 10th house ruler) and relationship patterns (Venus, Mars, 7th house, Juno). Each module cross-references your specific placements with house rulers and aspect patterns — producing original analysis, not recycled horoscope descriptions.

The analysis builds sequentially. Each section references what came before, so the final report reads as one coherent narrative — not 13 disconnected fragments.

Methodology

The astronomical layer uses Astrolog 7.80, a professional-grade engine powered by Swiss Ephemeris — the same ephemeris data used by research astrologers worldwide. We compute positions for 10 classical planets plus Chiron, Ceres, Pallas, Juno, Vesta, North Node, South Node, Black Moon Lilith, Vertex, and Part of Fortune. All 9 aspect types are calculated with exact orbs: conjunctions, sextiles, squares, trines, oppositions, semi-squares, sesquiquadrates, quincunxes, and semi-sextiles.

The interpretive layer draws on three established traditions: Liz Greene’s psychological astrology (depth psychology applied to chart patterns), Robert Hand’s systematic aspect analysis (precise delineation of planetary combinations), and Steven Forrest’s evolutionary astrology (growth-oriented interpretation of nodal axis and outer planets). We combine these frameworks with our own proprietary weighting system for aspect significance — factoring in orb tightness, planet type (personal vs. transpersonal), and house placement.

Astrology is not a science, and we don’t pretend otherwise. We don’t predict the future or claim certainty. What we offer is a rigorously structured framework for exploring personality patterns and life themes through the lens of your birth chart.

Founder

Founded by Vadik Iljin in Tallinn, Estonia. Background in data engineering, statistical analysis, and pattern recognition. The kind of person who sees a birth chart and thinks “what if I applied the same analytical rigor to interpretation that astronomers apply to calculation?”

Small operation, big attention to detail. Every report is individually written, checked for coherence, and verified before delivery.

Read the Substack →

ZodiacID

contact@zodiacid.com
Tallinn, Estonia

Get chart insights → Substack

For entertainment and self-reflection purposes only. Not a substitute for professional, medical, legal, or financial advice.

© 2026 ZodiacID

We use cookies for analytics to improve your experience.

Small operation, big attention to detail. Every report is individually written, checked for coherence, and verified before delivery.

content.text:
Confirmed — `cf/main` is at `4633d16`, our commit `185a92d` was only pushed to `origin` (zodiac-sites). Need to push to `cf`:

```
git push cf main
```

That will trigger the Cloudflare Pages deployment. The `cf` remote (`zodiacid/zodiacid.com`) is the one CF Pages watches — I mistakenly pushed to `origin` (`zodiac-sites`) which is unconnected to CF.

content.content:
1→# ZodiacID Project Memory
     2→
     3→## User Preferences
     4→- NEVER add "Co-Authored-By: Claude" to git commits
     5→- Website repo: github.com/zodiacid/zodiacid.com (static HTML, CF Pages, no build step)
     6→- Backend repo: github.com/zodiacid/zodiacid (Python API)
     7→
     8→## Critical Code Gaps (not in CLAUDE.md or skills)
     9→- Polar webhook only queues natal/synastry jobs (main.py:892-898) — needs red_flag/parenting_guide routing
    10→- No Sabian symbols data, no Arabic parts calculation
    11→- No leads table in schema.sql
    12→- No Cosmic ID Card (free lead magnet)
    13→- Transit calculation methods exist in calculator but no report generation flow
    14→
    15→## Current Pricing (updated 2026-02-17)
    16→- User Manual (natal): EUR 34.90
    17→- Relationship X-Ray (synastry): EUR 24.90

file.content:
# ZodiacID Project Memory

## User Preferences
- NEVER add "Co-Authored-By: Claude" to git commits
- Website repo: github.com/zodiacid/zodiacid.com (static HTML, CF Pages, no build step)
- Backend repo: github.com/zodiacid/zodiacid (Python API)

## Critical Code Gaps (not in CLAUDE.md or skills)
- Polar webhook only queues natal/synastry jobs (main.py:892-898) — needs red_flag/parenting_guide routing
- No Sabian symbols data, no Arabic parts calculation
- No leads table in schema.sql
- No Cosmic ID Card (free lead magnet)
- Transit calculation methods exist in calculator but no report generation flow

## Current Pricing (updated 2026-02-17)
- User Manual (natal): EUR 34.90
- Relationship X-Ray (synastry): EUR 24.90
- Parenting Guide: EUR 24.90
- Red Flag Check removed from website (code still exists, unlinked)
- No "Most Popular" badges, no "Founding Edition" branding

## Real Report Sizes (measured Feb 2026)
- Natal: ~40 pages, ~11K words, ~70K chars
- Synastry: ~40 pages, ~11K words, ~70K chars
- Red Flag: ~15 pages, ~3.5K words, ~25K chars
- Parenting: ~20 pages, ~5.3K words, ~34K chars
- User-facing copy uses PAGES not characters (chars sound AI-generated)
- vadikiljin.com not linked from site footer (not ready yet, plain text only)

## Implemented Products
- Red Flag Check: 3 analyzers (top_friction, fight_scenarios, survival_scripts) in `generation/red_flag/`
- Parenting Guide: 4 analyzers (emotional_needs, learning_style, talents, challenges) in `generation/parenting/`
- Both products use existing filters from `filters.py` and are routed via `service.py`
- Make targets: `make red_flag`, `make parenting`, `make report-red-flag-dry`, `make report-parenting-dry`

## TextPolisher (generation/openai/polisher.py)
- 2-level post-processing: FREE regex pre-fixes + conditional GPT-5-mini call
- Regex fixes: "Наблюдение:" removal (RU), "Заключительная честность:" → "Честный итог:" (RU), "See tähendab, et" removal (ET)
- Heuristic scan: long_paragraph (>4 sentences), code_switching (mixed Cyrillic/Latin), banned_phrase per language
- GPT response parser handles 4 formats: [N], N., N), **N**/### N + fallbacks
- Birth place localization: format_birth_info() accepts localized_place override, save_language_report() extracts from report dict
- 57 unit tests in tests/unit/test_polisher.py

## LLM Audit Reliability Warning
- Sonnet agents HALLUCINATE counting-based violations (e.g. claimed 47 paragraph violations, actual: 0)
- Always verify "N sentences" / "N paragraphs" claims with programmatic checks
- Audits are reliable for: banned words, structural patterns, language mixing, voice/tone
- Audits are unreliable for: counting, quantitative claims, exact line numbers

## Top Antipatterns
- service.py is a 1559-line god object (26 methods, R1 in refactor plan)
- 11 house analyzer files are identical boilerplate (~21 lines each, R2)
- openai_client.py is an empty wrapper re-exporting legacy_openai_client.py (R3)
- 3 methods duplicate natal generation (sync sequential, async parallel, pure parallel) (R4)
- database.py:38 has bare `except:` (catches KeyboardInterrupt!)

## Refactor Execution Order
R10 (git) -> R3 (wrapper) -> R7 (database) -> R2 (house files) -> R6 (constants) -> R1 (split service) -> R4 (async dedup) -> R5 (wire products) -> R9 (logging) -> R8 (test utils)
See `REFACTOR_PLAN.md` for details.

## Public Positioning / Substack Voice
- Vadik = astrologer with data science background (NOT tech founder building a product)
- Never mention pipelines, automation, LLMs, or AI generation in public-facing copy
- Frame everything as manual expert work: "written, proofread, checked by hand"
- Technical authority: Astrolog 7.80 / Swiss Ephemeris, 20 celestial bodies, 50+ aspects, precise orbs
- Analytical framework: statistical signature (planetary weights, elemental distribution, aspect geometry, dispositorship chains)
- Interpretive frameworks: Liz Greene (psychological), Robert Hand (systematic), Steven Forrest (evolutionary)
- 13 report sections written individually per placement
- Substack "Pattern Recognition" = charts + data + astrology intersection

## User Preferences

input.new_string:
## User Preferences
- NEVER add "Co-Authored-By: Claude" to git commits
- Website repo: github.com/zodiacid/zodiacid.com (static HTML, CF Pages, no build step)
- Backend repo: github.com/zodiacid/zodiacid (Python API)

## CRITICAL: Website Deployment (sites/zodiacid)
- sites/zodiacid has TWO remotes: `origin` (zodiac-sites) and `cf` (zodiacid.com)
- Cloudflare Pages watches the `cf` remote (zodiacid/zodiacid.com) — NOT `origin`
- ALWAYS push to `cf`: `git push cf main` — pushing to `origin` does NOTHING for deployment
- NEVER use bare `git push` in sites/zodiacid — always specify `cf` explicitly

data.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" | grep -qE '(service|openai_client)\.py'; then echo 'Revenue-critical file edited. Run: make validate'; fi

content.text: The issue is the `cd` + `&&` chaining doesn't match the `git push *` pattern. Let me add explicit rules for `cd` + git push combos and also for git commands with `-C` flag:

input.new_string:
"Bash(git push *)",
      "Bash(git push * --force)",
      "Bash(cd sites/zodiacid && git push *)",
      "Bash(git -C sites/zodiacid push *)"

data.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" | grep -qE '(service|openai_client)\.py'; then echo 'Revenue-critical file edited. Run: make validate'; fi
